entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Legume variety ICC 4948 used to study nodulation capacities.

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Crop grown following chickpea in a cropping system.

entities.name: NODULATION CAPACITY
entities.description: The ability of chickpea plants to form nodules for nitrogen fixation, categorized as high or low.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The measure of grain production in chickpea.

entities.name: ATMOSPHERIC NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The process of converting atmospheric nitrogen into a usable form by plants and microbes.

entities.name: ACETYLENE REDUCTION ACTIVITY
entities.description: A technique used to measure the rate of atmospheric nitrogen fixation.

entities.name: LOW SOIL NITROGEN
entities.description: A soil environment characterized by low levels of mineral nitrogen.

entities.name: MICROBIAL ACTIVITY
entities.description: The biological activity of microorganisms in the soil surrounding the roots.

entities.name: RHIZOSPHERE
entities.description: The area of soil immediately surrounding the plant roots.

entities.name: FLUSH C:N RATIO
entities.description: The ratio of carbon to nitrogen in the soil flush, indicating nutrient availability.

entities.name: IRRIGATED CONDITIONS
entities.description: Agricultural conditions where water is artificially applied to the land.

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The process by which microorganisms convert atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia.

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The overall yield and efficiency of the cropping system over time.

entities.name: CHICKPEA-SORGHUM CROPPING SYSTEM
entities.description: A sequential planting system involving chickpea followed by sorghum.

relationships.description: promotes higher fixation in high nodulating selections
high nodulating selections result in a narrower ratio
narrower ratio increases nitrogen availability for
